preview_text:
The model rates Netherlands as a clear favorite with a 68% win probability, leaving Sweden with only an 8% chance of victory. The spread reflects a substantial gap in the statistical expectation, though the underdog retains non-trivial odds of an upset result.

The draw emerges as a secondary outcome in both model and market estimates, sitting at 24%. This suggests the match carries meaningful competitive structure—Netherlands starts favored but neither team is heavily favored to dominate play. The 24-point gap between the favorite and underdog indicates asymmetry, yet the presence of a sizeable draw probability prevents the matchup from collapsing into a one-sided proposition.

Model and market diverge notably on the underdog. The sportsbooks assign Sweden 19% win probability versus the model's 8%, a gap of 11 percentage points. Sportsbooks also compress Netherlands' win rate to 56% from 68%. Both sources align tightly on the draw at roughly 24-25%. The dashboard does not adjudicate which source is more accurate; the divergence merits attention from those assessing betting value.
